# Implementation Plan: `ch32-workbench` v1

**Input PRD:** [ch32-workbench-setup-prd.md](ch32-workbench-setup-prd.md)
**Author:** plan generated by Claude
**Status:** ready for execution

This plan is the concrete, file-by-file execution roadmap for the v1 scaffold described in the PRD. It captures decisions reached in the planning conversation, lists every artifact to be produced, sequences the work into phases, and defines what "done" looks like.

---

## 0. PRD changes applied (changelog)

These adjustments were agreed during planning and have been folded **into the PRD itself** — this section is a changelog, not a list of pending overrides. The PRD is the source of truth; this list exists so reviewers can see what shifted between PRD v1.0 and the version this plan targets.

1. **§2 Non-Goals — CI line removed.** v1 now ships a *minimal* GitHub Actions CI (build matrix + size report + shellcheck + clang-format). Deeper static analysis (cppcheck, clang-tidy, `-fanalyzer`) and HIL remain explicitly out of scope.
2. **§4 layout — added** `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, `ci/{README.md,size-report.sh}`, and `.clang-format` at repo root.
3. **§7 Per-app Makefile template** — added explicit `CFLAGS += -Wextra` so the README's stated warning level holds regardless of upstream ch32fun.mk defaults.
4. **§8.1** — codified the include-order contract: apps include `ch32fun.h` first, then `board.h`. Board headers do NOT include ch32fun. `boards/board.h` documents this in a leading comment block. `.clang-format` sets `SortIncludes: false` to keep tooling from breaking it.
5. **§8.3 board table** — j4m6 minimal grew a button (PD4, active-low, user-wired, `TODO(verify):`) so `gpio_button` builds for all three boards. Devboard and qfn20 also got explicit button columns (PC1, active-low).
6. **§13 README** — added a WSL2 subsection covering `usbipd-win` attach, and a 6th troubleshooting entry for libusb-header failures when `setup.sh` is skipped.
7. **§12 `.gitignore`** — now also ignores `*.s` and `*.su`.
8. **§6 top-level Makefile** — `BOARD=` supplied at the root propagates to every recursive app build; if unset, each app uses its `DEFAULT_BOARD`.
9. **New §15 "CI / Quality Gates (Minimal)"** inserted. Original §15/§16/§17 renumbered to §16/§17/§18 (and the FreeRTOS subsection from §16.1 → §17.1). All internal cross-references updated.
10. **§16 Acceptance Criteria** gained a 12th item: CI must run green on push.

Decisions captured from planning:

- **ch32fun pin**: current `master` HEAD at scaffold time.
- **LICENSE holder**: "ch32-workbench contributors".
- **udev rules style**: `MODE="0660", TAG+="uaccess"` (preferred over world-writable `0666`).
- **`scripts/setup.sh` will be executed** as the final verification step, so the agent itself confirms the build works end-to-end (sans hardware).

---

## 1. Work sequencing

Eight phases. Each phase has a clear exit criterion AND a regression check; do not move on until both pass.

| Phase | Goal | Exit criterion | Regression check |
|---|---|---|---|
| **P1. Submodule + skeleton** | ch32fun pinned, all directories exist, .gitignore in place | `git submodule status` shows the pinned SHA; `find . -type d` matches PRD §4 layout | n/a (first phase) |
| **P2. Build glue** | Top-level + per-app Makefiles working; `board.h` umbrella resolves | A *stub* `blink.c` (empty `main`) builds cleanly: produces `blink.elf`, `blink.bin`, `blink.hex` with no warnings | P1 exit still holds |
| **P3a. Board headers** | All three board headers exist with full `BOARD_*` macro sets; pin choices documented in leading comment blocks | The P2 stub compiles for *every* board: `make BOARD=ch32v003f4p6_devboard`, `…j4m6_minimal`, `…a4m6_qfn20` all produce ELFs cleanly | P1 + P2 exits still hold |
| **P3b. blink app** | Real `blink.c` implemented using `BOARD_LED_*` macros only | `blink.elf` builds cleanly for all three boards | P1 + P2 + P3a exits still hold |
| **P3c. uart_hello app** | `uart_hello.c` implemented; sends greeting via USART1 polling | `uart_hello.elf` builds cleanly for all three boards | P1 + P2 + P3a + P3b exits still hold; blink still builds for all 3 boards |
| **P3d. gpio_button app** | `gpio_button.c` implemented with debounce; `#if !BOARD_HAS_BUTTON` guard present | `gpio_button.elf` builds cleanly for all three boards; top-level `make` builds all 3 apps × 3 boards | P1 + P2 + P3a + P3b + P3c exits still hold |
| **P4. Setup + udev + README** | `./scripts/setup.sh` is idempotent; udev rules in place; README satisfies every PRD §13 item | `setup.sh` finishes cleanly on the agent's host; README quick-start works verbatim; top-level `make clean && make` succeeds | All prior phase exits still hold |
| **P5. CI** | GitHub Actions builds all apps × boards on Ubuntu 22.04 + 24.04; size report; shellcheck; clang-format check | A push to a scratch branch produces a green run on both runners | All prior phase exits still hold; **lint job may surface format violations in code from P3a–P3d; fix those in-place before exit, then re-run all regression checks** |

**Commit policy (mandatory):** one commit per phase, no exceptions. Commit messages follow this pattern: `Pn: <short summary>` (e.g. `P3b: implement blink with BOARD_LED_* macros`). Eight phases = eight commits. Each commit is an independent rollback point. PRD §18.3 explicitly allows multi-commit history; this plan exercises that allowance.

**Regression check definition:** before staging a commit for phase Pn, re-run every prior phase's exit criterion as a quick sanity loop. Any failure blocks the commit — diagnose and fix in-place rather than committing broken state. This is what catches "I broke an earlier app while writing a later one."

---

## 2. Phase 1 — Submodule + skeleton

### 2.1 Submodule

```bash
git submodule add https://github.com/cnlohr/ch32fun.git ch32fun
git -C ch32fun checkout master
git submodule update --init --recursive
```

Record the resulting SHA. The README's "Toolchain versions" section will quote it.

**Verification**: inspect the submodule layout to determine the actual path to `ch32fun.mk`. Two known possibilities (per PRD §7):

- `ch32fun/ch32fun/ch32fun.mk` (current upstream as of recent commits) — per-app Makefile uses `CH32FUN := ../../ch32fun/ch32fun`.
- `ch32fun/ch32v003fun/ch32v003fun.mk` (older naming) — adjust path accordingly.

Whichever is present in the pinned commit dictates the include path in every app Makefile.

### 2.2 Directory tree

Create all directories from §4. Empty directories get `.gitkeep`:

- `common/.gitkeep`

### 2.3 `.gitignore` (final form)

```
*.o
*.elf
*.bin
*.hex
*.lst
*.map
*.d
*.s
*.su
build/
compile_commands.json
.cache/
```

### 2.4 `.gitmodules`

Auto-generated by `git submodule add`. Verify it pins the URL and path correctly; nothing else to do.

### 2.5 `LICENSE`

Standard MIT, copyright line: `Copyright (c) 2026 ch32-workbench contributors`.

**Exit P1** when the tree matches PRD §4 (minus content of code/Makefile/README files, which are P2–P4) and the submodule is pinned.

**Regression check:** n/a (P1 is the first phase).

**Commit:** `P1: submodule + skeleton (ch32fun pinned at <sha>)`.

---

## 3. Phase 2 — Build glue

### 3.1 Top-level `Makefile`

Sketch:

```make
APPS := $(notdir $(patsubst %/,%,$(wildcard apps/*/)))
BOARD_HEADERS := $(filter-out boards/board.h,$(wildcard boards/*.h))
BOARDS := $(notdir $(BOARD_HEADERS:.h=))

# Propagate BOARD only if user set it explicitly (override default).
ifdef BOARD
  MAKE_BOARD_ARG := BOARD=$(BOARD)
endif

.PHONY: all clean list-apps list-boards help

all:
	@for d in $(APPS); do $(MAKE) -C apps/$$d $(MAKE_BOARD_ARG) || exit $$?; done

clean:
	@for d in $(APPS); do $(MAKE) -C apps/$$d clean || exit $$?; done

list-apps:
	@printf '%s\n' $(APPS)

list-boards:
	@printf '%s\n' $(BOARDS)

help:
	@echo "Targets:"
	@echo "  make                   - build all apps for their default boards"
	@echo "  make BOARD=<name>      - build all apps for the named board"
	@echo "  make clean             - remove all build artifacts"
	@echo "  make list-apps         - list discovered apps"
	@echo "  make list-boards       - list discovered board headers"
	@echo ""
	@echo "Per-app:"
	@echo "  cd apps/<name> && make [BOARD=<name>]"
	@echo "  cd apps/<name> && make flash"
```

### 3.2 Per-app `Makefile` (template, used by all three apps)

```make
TARGET        := <name>
TARGET_MCU    := CH32V003
DEFAULT_BOARD ?= ch32v003f4p6_devboard
BOARD         ?= $(DEFAULT_BOARD)

CH32FUN := ../../ch32fun/ch32fun   # adjust per actual upstream layout

CFLAGS  += -I../../boards -DBOARD_$(shell echo $(BOARD) | tr a-z A-Z) -Wextra

ADDITIONAL_C_FILES :=

include $(CH32FUN)/ch32fun.mk

flash : cv_flash
clean : cv_clean
```

`-Wextra` added explicitly so the README claim holds regardless of upstream defaults.

### 3.3 `boards/board.h` (umbrella)

Exact content from PRD §8.1, plus a leading comment block:

```c
/*
 * boards/board.h — board selection umbrella.
 *
 * Contract: apps MUST include "ch32fun.h" BEFORE including this header,
 * so that GPIOx, RCC_APB2Periph_GPIOx, etc. are already in scope.
 * Per-board headers (e.g. ch32v003f4p6_devboard.h) only define BOARD_*
 * macros and do not include ch32fun themselves.
 */
```

### 3.4 Smoke test

Once `boards/board.h` and one per-app Makefile exist, write a stub `apps/blink/blink.c` (just `int main(void){while(1);}` plus the right headers) and verify it links. This is the earliest signal that the toolchain + include paths are correctly wired. Real `blink.c` lands in P3.

**Exit P2** when the stub produces `blink.elf` / `blink.bin` / `blink.hex` cleanly (default board only; multi-board verification is P3a's job).

**Regression check:** P1 exit still holds (`git submodule status` shows pinned SHA, directory tree intact).

**Commit:** `P2: top-level + per-app Makefile, board.h umbrella, blink stub`.

> Note: the stub `blink.c` written in P2 is overwritten by the real implementation in P3b. It exists only to validate the toolchain end-to-end in P2 and to serve as the multi-board compile target in P3a.

---

## 4. Phase 3 (a–d) — Board headers and sample apps

Phase 3 is split into four commits so each app is a separate, rollback-safe step. Coding standards (§4.0) apply to every sub-phase.

### 4.0 Coding standards (applies to all of P3a–P3d)

- C11, 4-space indent, no tabs in our code.
- One blank line at EOF.
- No `<stdio.h>` in any of the three apps.
- Header guards match `#ifndef BOARD_<NAME>_H` pattern in board headers.
- Apps include `ch32fun.h` *before* `board.h` (see PRD §8.1 contract).

### 4a — Phase 3a: Board headers

Three header files, all following the PRD §8.2 macro convention. Each starts with a comment block describing the physical wiring assumed.

| File | LED | Button | UART TX | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `ch32v003f4p6_devboard.h` | PD6, active high | PC1, active low (`BOARD_HAS_BUTTON 1`) | PD5 @ 115200 | Common AliExpress dev board; agent verifies wiring against PRD §8.3 |
| `ch32v003j4m6_minimal.h` | PC1, active high | PD4, active low (`BOARD_HAS_BUTTON 1`) | PD5 @ 115200 | Bare J4M6 on perfboard; button is user-wired on a free SOP-8 GPIO. `TODO(verify):` on both LED and button pins |
| `ch32v003a4m6_qfn20.h` | PD6, active high | PC1, active low (`BOARD_HAS_BUTTON 1`) | PD5 @ 115200 | QFN-20 variant; `TODO(verify):` likewise |

All three boards declare `BOARD_HAS_BUTTON 1` — so `gpio_button` will build for every supported board in P3d. The `#if !BOARD_HAS_BUTTON` guard in `gpio_button.c` is forward compatibility for future headers, not a gate against any v1 board.

Also write `boards/README.md` here, since the board pattern is what's being established.

**P3a exit:** the P2 stub `blink.c` (empty `main`) compiles cleanly for *every* board:

```
make -C apps/blink BOARD=ch32v003f4p6_devboard
make -C apps/blink BOARD=ch32v003j4m6_minimal
make -C apps/blink BOARD=ch32v003a4m6_qfn20
```

All three produce ELFs with no warnings. This validates the board-header pattern before any real app code is written.

**P3a regression check:** P1 + P2 exits still hold.

**Commit:** `P3a: add three board headers (devboard, j4m6_minimal, a4m6_qfn20)`.

### 4b — Phase 3b: `apps/blink/blink.c`

Replace the P2 stub with a real implementation:

- Includes `ch32fun.h` then `board.h`.
- `SystemInit()` from ch32fun.
- `RCC->APB2PCENR |= BOARD_LED_RCC_BIT;` (or the appropriate enable macro).
- Configures `BOARD_LED_PORT` pin `BOARD_LED_PIN` as push-pull output (10 MHz is fine).
- Loop: toggle pin, `Delay_Ms(250)` → ~2 Hz visible blink.
- Respect `BOARD_LED_ACTIVE_HIGH` in the initial state.

**P3b exit:** `blink.elf` builds cleanly for all three boards.

**P3b regression check:** P1 + P2 + P3a still hold (P3a still passes with the *new* blink, not the stub).

**Commit:** `P3b: implement blink with BOARD_LED_* macros`.

### 4c — Phase 3c: `apps/uart_hello/uart_hello.c`

- Configures USART1 on `BOARD_UART_TX_*` at `BOARD_UART_BAUD`.
- No `printf`; a tiny `uart_write(const char *s)` that pushes bytes to `USART1->DATAR` after polling `USART_FLAG_TXE`.
- Builds the greeting via two string literals + the `BOARD_NAME` / `BOARD_MCU` macros — no `snprintf`.
- 1 Hz loop via `Delay_Ms(1000)`.

**P3c exit:** `uart_hello.elf` builds cleanly for all three boards.

**P3c regression check:** P1 + P2 + P3a + P3b still hold; specifically, blink still builds for all 3 boards.

**Commit:** `P3c: implement uart_hello with polled USART1`.

### 4d — Phase 3d: `apps/gpio_button/gpio_button.c`

- `#if !BOARD_HAS_BUTTON` → `#error "This app requires BOARD_HAS_BUTTON"`.
- Configures button pin as input with pull-up if `BOARD_BUTTON_ACTIVE_LOW`, pull-down otherwise.
- Configures LED as in blink.
- Loop: read button, software debounce (sample twice, 15 ms apart), mirror onto LED honoring both `BOARD_LED_ACTIVE_HIGH` and `BOARD_BUTTON_ACTIVE_LOW`.

**P3d exit:**

- `gpio_button.elf` builds cleanly for all three boards.
- Top-level `make` builds all 3 apps for their `DEFAULT_BOARD`.
- Top-level `make BOARD=ch32v003j4m6_minimal` builds all 3 apps for the alternate board.
- Top-level `make clean` removes all build artifacts.

**P3d regression check:** P1 + P2 + P3a + P3b + P3c still hold; blink and uart_hello still build for all 3 boards.

**Commit:** `P3d: implement gpio_button with debounce + BOARD_HAS_BUTTON guard`.

---

## 5. Phase 4 — Setup, udev, README

### 5.1 `scripts/setup.sh`

`#!/usr/bin/env bash` with `set -euo pipefail`. Behavior strictly per PRD §10. Idempotency strategy:

- `apt-get install -y` is naturally idempotent.
- udev rules: install to `/etc/udev/rules.d/60-wch-link.rules` via `install -m 0644`; safe to overwrite.
- Reload + trigger commands always run (cheap and idempotent).
- Distro check: parse `ID` and `ID_LIKE` from `/etc/os-release`; accept `ubuntu`, `debian`. Anything else: print a clear message ("This script targets Ubuntu/Debian; on other distros, install equivalents of: …") and exit non-zero.

### 5.2 `scripts/60-wch-link.rules`

```
# WCH-LinkE programmer
SUBSYSTEM=="usb", ATTR{idVendor}=="1a86", ATTR{idProduct}=="8010", MODE="0660", TAG+="uaccess"
SUBSYSTEM=="usb", ATTR{idVendor}=="1a86", ATTR{idProduct}=="8012", MODE="0660", TAG+="uaccess"
SUBSYSTEM=="hidraw", ATTRS{idVendor}=="1a86", ATTRS{idProduct}=="8010", MODE="0660", TAG+="uaccess"
SUBSYSTEM=="hidraw", ATTRS{idVendor}=="1a86", ATTRS{idProduct}=="8012", MODE="0660", TAG+="uaccess"

# CH32V003 in BOOT0 / factory ISP mode
SUBSYSTEM=="usb", ATTR{idVendor}=="4348", ATTR{idProduct}=="55e0", MODE="0660", TAG+="uaccess"
```

### 5.3 `README.md`

Sections in the order specified by PRD §13, plus:

- **WSL2 subsection** under "Programmer setup" explaining `usbipd-win`:
  ```
  wsl --list -v                      # confirm WSL2 distro name
  usbipd list                        # find WCH-LinkE bus-id
  usbipd bind --busid <id>
  usbipd attach --wsl --busid <id>   # repeat after every reboot/replug
  ```
- **Troubleshooting** entries (6 per PRD §13 item 8):
  1. Chip not detected → check 3V3, GND, SWIO wiring; press programmer's button.
  2. `Permission denied` on `/dev/hidraw*` → udev rules not loaded or user not in active seat (relog).
  3. Wrong board selected → blink/UART silent → confirm `BOARD=...` matches the physical board.
  4. Stale submodule → `git submodule update --init --recursive` after a pull.
  5. BOOT0 stuck (chip enters ISP every reset) → hold BOOT0 low while powering up only when intentionally entering ISP.
  6. First `make flash` fails on libusb headers → user skipped `./scripts/setup.sh`.

### 5.4 Top-level smoke test

Run `./scripts/setup.sh` (will require sudo password from the user/host). Then from repo root: `make`. Expected: three ELFs build with no warnings. Then `make clean`; verify artifacts are gone.

**Exit P4** when setup.sh completes cleanly on the agent's host and top-level `make` succeeds.

**Regression check:** all P1–P3d exits still hold. Specifically: every (app × board) combo from P3d still builds cleanly; the README quick-start commands match the actual behavior of the Makefiles written in P2–P3d.

**Commit:** `P4: setup script, udev rules, README`.

---

## 6. Phase 5 — CI (Minimal tier)

### 6.1 `.github/workflows/ci.yml`

One workflow, two jobs:

**Job A: `build`** (matrix: Ubuntu 22.04 + 24.04)

Steps:
1. `actions/checkout@v4` with `submodules: recursive`.
2. Install toolchain: `sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y gcc-riscv64-unknown-elf build-essential libusb-1.0-0-dev libudev-dev pkg-config`. (We deliberately do NOT run `setup.sh` — CI shouldn't install udev rules; it just needs the build deps.)
3. For each app, for each board, run `make -C apps/<app> BOARD=<board>`. Implementation: a small shell loop driven by `ls apps/` and `ls boards/*.h` (filtered to exclude `board.h`). Every (app × board) combination must succeed — all three v1 boards declare `BOARD_HAS_BUTTON 1`, so there are no expected failures. If any combination fails, the job fails.
4. `./ci/size-report.sh` — produces a markdown table of flash/SRAM use per ELF, appended to `$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY`.
5. Upload built ELFs as a workflow artifact (handy for size comparisons across PRs).

**Job B: `lint`** (single Ubuntu 24.04 runner)

Steps:
1. Checkout.
2. `shellcheck scripts/setup.sh ci/*.sh`.
3. `clang-format --dry-run --Werror $(find apps boards common -name '*.c' -o -name '*.h')` — fails CI if anything is unformatted.

### 6.2 `ci/size-report.sh`

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
printf '| App | Board | Flash | SRAM |\n|---|---|---:|---:|\n'
for elf in apps/*/*.elf; do
  app=$(basename "$(dirname "$elf")")
  # size output: text data bss → flash = text+data, sram = data+bss
  read -r text data bss _ < <(riscv64-unknown-elf-size "$elf" | tail -n1)
  flash=$((text + data))
  sram=$((data + bss))
  printf '| %s | %s | %d B | %d B |\n' "$app" "${BOARD:-default}" "$flash" "$sram"
done
```

(Plan calls for refining this so each row also captures which BOARD the ELF was built for — likely by stashing per-build size snapshots into a temp dir during the matrix loop rather than scraping after the fact. Final shape determined during implementation.)

### 6.3 `.clang-format`

Start from `BasedOnStyle: LLVM` with overrides:

```yaml
BasedOnStyle: LLVM
IndentWidth: 4
UseTab: Never
ColumnLimit: 100
AlignConsecutiveMacros: AcrossEmptyLines
AllowShortFunctionsOnASingleLine: None
SortIncludes: false
```

(SortIncludes off because include order matters: `ch32fun.h` must come before `board.h`.)

### 6.4 `ci/README.md`

One-liner per helper script so CI internals stay discoverable.

**Exit P5** when CI runs green on a push to a scratch branch (or via `act` locally, agent's choice).

**Regression check:** all P1–P4 exits still hold. Notable risk: the `clang-format --dry-run --Werror` lint will surface any pre-existing format violations in code written during P3a–P3d. Fix those in-place *in this phase* (do not amend earlier commits), re-run all prior phase exit checks to confirm nothing else broke, then commit.

**Commit:** `P5: CI workflow + size report + clang-format`.

> If lint forces re-formatting of files from P3a–P3d, the format-cleanup goes into the P5 commit, not into amendments of P3 commits. This keeps the per-phase history honest.

---

## 7. Verification matrix (maps to PRD §16)

| PRD criterion | How verified in this plan |
|---|---|
| 1. `git clone --recurse-submodules` works | Implicit — submodule is properly registered with public URL |
| 2. `setup.sh` runs to completion | Phase 4 smoke test on agent's host |
| 3. `apps/blink && make` produces ELF/bin/hex, no warnings | Phase 2 smoke test + Phase 3 final |
| 4. `make BOARD=ch32v003j4m6_minimal` rebuilds | Phase 3 exit criterion |
| 5. `make flash` programs the board (LED blinks) | **Requires hardware** — agent reports as unverified, build portion confirmed |
| 6. `uart_hello` shows hello via UART | **Requires hardware** — build portion confirmed |
| 7. `gpio_button` mirrors button → LED | **Requires hardware** — build portion confirmed |
| 8. Top-level `make` builds all apps | Phase 3 exit + Phase 4 smoke test |
| 9. Top-level `make clean` removes artifacts | Phase 4 smoke test |
| 10. `make list-apps` / `make list-boards` correct | Phase 2 |
| 11. README quick-start works verbatim | Phase 4 — agent walks through it on a clean checkout |
| 12. CI runs green on push | Phase 5 — push to a scratch branch, observe green run |

---

## 8. Risks & open items

- **ch32fun upstream layout drift.** The path to `ch32fun.mk` and the names of variables it consumes have shifted historically. Phase 1 must inspect the pinned commit and reflect the *actual* layout in app Makefiles. If `TARGET_MCU=CH32V003` doesn't match what upstream expects in the pinned commit, the plan changes.
- **Board wiring on j4m6 / a4m6 variants.** PRD §8.3 acknowledges the agent cannot verify these without hardware. Plan: ship them with `TODO(verify):` comments and accurate-to-the-best-of-knowledge pin choices, so users who own the parts can correct in a follow-up PR.
- **`-Wextra` cleanliness for ch32fun-supplied headers.** If ch32fun's headers trigger `-Wextra` warnings on user code via macro expansion, may need to suppress with `-Wno-...` selectively. Decide during P3.
- **WSL2 host running setup.sh.** Agent's environment is WSL2 per system context. `sudo apt-get` will need a password; udev reload on WSL2 may behave differently than on bare Linux. Plan: run setup.sh; if udev reload fails noisily on WSL2, document it in README rather than work around it.
- **CI size report fidelity.** The sketch in §6.2 only reports the *last* build per app (ELFs get overwritten across the matrix loop). Final implementation will stash per-build sizes during the matrix iteration, not scrape after.
- **Future FreeRTOS variant** (PRD §17.1). v1 plan creates nothing under `third_party/` and adds no `USE_FREERTOS` plumbing. The only forward-compat hook is the repo's *name* (`ch32-workbench`, not `ch32-baremetal`) and the per-app `TARGET_MCU` convention.

---

```
ch32-workbench/
├── .clang-format                            [NEW, P5]
├── .github/workflows/ci.yml                 [NEW, P5]
├── .gitignore                               [NEW, P1]
├── .gitmodules                              [NEW, P1 (auto)]
├── LICENSE                                  [NEW, P1]
├── Makefile                                 [NEW, P2]
├── README.md                                [NEW, P4]
├── apps/
│   ├── blink/Makefile                       [NEW, P2]
│   ├── blink/blink.c                        [NEW, P2 stub → P3b real implementation]
│   ├── uart_hello/{Makefile,uart_hello.c}   [NEW, P3c]
│   └── gpio_button/{Makefile,gpio_button.c} [NEW, P3d]
├── boards/
│   ├── README.md                            [NEW, P3a]
│   ├── board.h                              [NEW, P2]
│   ├── ch32v003f4p6_devboard.h              [NEW, P3a]
│   ├── ch32v003j4m6_minimal.h               [NEW, P3a]
│   └── ch32v003a4m6_qfn20.h                 [NEW, P3a]
├── ch32fun/                                 [submodule, P1]
├── ci/
│   ├── README.md                            [NEW, P5]
│   └── size-report.sh                       [NEW, P5]
├── common/.gitkeep                          [NEW, P1]
├── docs/
│   ├── ch32-workbench-setup-prd.md          [pre-existing]
│   └── ch32-workbench-setup-plan.md         [this file]
└── scripts/
    ├── 60-wch-link.rules                    [NEW, P4]
    └── setup.sh                             [NEW, P4]
```

Total new files (excluding the submodule's own contents): **22**, plus this plan and the existing PRD under `docs/`.

### 9.1 Commit ledger (8 commits, one per phase)

| # | Commit message | Files touched |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `P1: submodule + skeleton (ch32fun pinned at <sha>)` | `.gitignore`, `.gitmodules`, `LICENSE`, `ch32fun/`, `common/.gitkeep`, all empty dirs |
| 2 | `P2: top-level + per-app Makefile, board.h umbrella, blink stub` | `Makefile`, `apps/blink/Makefile`, `apps/blink/blink.c` (stub), `boards/board.h` |
| 3 | `P3a: add three board headers (devboard, j4m6_minimal, a4m6_qfn20)` | `boards/README.md`, `boards/ch32v003*.h` (×3) |
| 4 | `P3b: implement blink with BOARD_LED_* macros` | `apps/blink/blink.c` (replaces stub) |
| 5 | `P3c: implement uart_hello with polled USART1` | `apps/uart_hello/{Makefile,uart_hello.c}` |
| 6 | `P3d: implement gpio_button with debounce + BOARD_HAS_BUTTON guard` | `apps/gpio_button/{Makefile,gpio_button.c}` |
| 7 | `P4: setup script, udev rules, README` | `README.md`, `scripts/{setup.sh,60-wch-link.rules}` |
| 8 | `P5: CI workflow + size report + clang-format` | `.clang-format`, `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, `ci/{README.md,size-report.sh}` (+ any P3 format fixes lint surfaces) |

---

**End of plan.**
